title: Assistant turns silently dropped from conversation context — dict vs SDK-object mismatch in extractors
what_failed: Pi could not see its own prior replies. extract_text_from_messages and _build_context only captured content blocks via `hasattr(block, 'text')` (true for Anthropic SDK objects) or a tool_result dict. But _build_assistant_content stores EVERY assistant turn as a canonical dict {'type':'text','text':...}, which has no `.text` attribute and is not a tool_result — so it matched neither branch and was discarded. The `.text` branch was effectively dead code (nothing stores SDK objects anymore). Net effect: all three context paths saw only the user's half of the conversation.
where_failed: agent/truncation.py extract_text_from_messages (normie session_ctx + session summary) and _build_context (root compression input). Storage shape set by pi_agent.py:709 _build_assistant_content.
why_likely: Refactor rot. The R8/router work (T-089) changed self.messages to store provider-portable dicts via _build_assistant_content, but the two extractor helpers were never updated from the older SDK-object assumption. No test used the dict shape (test_compression_fallback used plain 'msg {i}' strings), so verify stayed green.
impact_sites: normie in-session context (single_message_ctx → session_ctx_inject is its ONLY memory)
root-mode history compression summary (_build_context feeds the summarizer)
session-exit summary → L2 distillation → long-term memory (generate_session_summary)
migration_plan: 1. Add shared _block_text(block) helper handling SDK objects, {'type':'text'} dicts, and tool_result dicts. [done]
3. Guard truncate_messages_safely against returning [] when no string-user boundary exists in the tail (backward-walk fallback, then full-list). [done]
4. Add testing/test_context_fidelity.py storing messages in the REAL production shape; assert assistant replies survive. [done]
implementation_notes: Fixed + verified in this session. verify.py PASS (218 files, 93 tests, 0 failures). Structural follow-up (normie's tiny window even with the fix) is T-149; T-143 updated with the corrected root cause; missing integration coverage is T-152.
